de.uka.ilkd.key.smt

Class Pipe<T>

  • java.lang.Object
    • de.uka.ilkd.key.smt.Pipe<T>

  • class Pipe<T>
extends Object
    On each side of the pipe there are sender and receivers: Receiver ====<=Output======= Sender ****************** KeY* Sender ======Input=>====== Receiver *External Process* Receiver ====<=Error======== Sender ******************

    • Nested Class Summary

      Nested Classes 
      Modifier and Type Class and Description
      private class  Pipe.Receiver
      Waits until a message is received from the other side of the pipe.
      private class  Pipe.Sender
      Mainly consists of a queue for messages.
      private class  Pipe.Worker
      Worker class for both the receiver and sender of the pipe.

    • Field Summary

      Fields 
      Modifier and Type Field and Description
      static int ERROR_MESSAGE 
      private ReentrantLock listenerLock 
      private LinkedList<PipeListener<T>> listeners 
      private String[] messageDelimiters
      The delimiters of the messages, i.e.
      static int NORMAL_MESSAGE 
      private Condition postMessages
      The sender goes to sleep when there are no messages to be sent.
      private LinkedList<String> queue
      Stores messages that are to be sent in the next cycle of the worker responsible for sending messages.
      private ReentrantLock queueLock 
      private T session
      User specific data.
      private ReentrantLock workerLock 
      private List<Pipe.Worker> workers
      The workers of the pipe.

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      Pipe(T session, String[] messageDelimiters) 

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      (package private) void addListener(PipeListener<T> listener) 
      void close() 
      (package private) void closeSendingPipe()
      Closes the pipe for sending, signalling the end of the input to the target process.
      T getSession() 
      (package private) void sendMessage(String message)
      sends a message to the external process.
      void start(InputStream input, OutputStream output, InputStream error, PipeListener<T> listener) 
      void waitForPipe()
      Wait until the output of the target process has been read completely.

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

    • Field Detail

      • listenerLock

        private final ReentrantLock listenerLock

      • listeners

        private final LinkedList<PipeListener<T>> listeners

      • workers

        private List<Pipe.Worker> workers
        The workers of the pipe. One worker is responsible for sending messages, while the other two workers handle messages which are received.

      • workerLock

        private final ReentrantLock workerLock

      • queue

        private LinkedList<String> queue
        Stores messages that are to be sent in the next cycle of the worker responsible for sending messages.

      • queueLock

        private final ReentrantLock queueLock

      • postMessages

        private final Condition postMessages
        The sender goes to sleep when there are no messages to be sent. If you want to wake him up, use this condition.

      • messageDelimiters

        private final String[] messageDelimiters
        The delimiters of the messages, i.e. strings that indicate the end of a message. If you specify several delimiters a single message is chosen as small as possible, i.e., it does not contain any delimiter.

      • NORMAL_MESSAGE

        public static final int NORMAL_MESSAGE
        See Also:
        Constant Field Values

      • ERROR_MESSAGE

        public static final int ERROR_MESSAGE
        See Also:
        Constant Field Values

      • session

        private final T session
        User specific data.

    • Constructor Detail

      • Pipe

        public Pipe(T session,
            String[] messageDelimiters)

    • Method Detail

      • start

        public void start(InputStream input,
                  OutputStream output,
                  InputStream error,
                  PipeListener<T> listener)

      • close

        public void close()

      • sendMessage

        void sendMessage(String message)
        sends a message to the external process. Mainly it adds a new message to the message queue and wakes up the sender.

      • closeSendingPipe

        void closeSendingPipe()
        Closes the pipe for sending, signalling the end of the input to the target process.

      • addListener

        void addListener(PipeListener<T> listener)

      • getSession

        public T getSession()

      • waitForPipe

        public void waitForPipe()
                 throws InterruptedException
        Wait until the output of the target process has been read completely.
        Throws:
        InterruptedException
